name: Holo ASCII
summary: Type anything, get ASCII art. A native text→ASCII (FIGlet) encoder with 328 fonts — byte-identical to the TAAG tool, no CDN, fonts verified on read.

description.p: A native text-to-ASCII-art encoder for Hologram, built on the FIGfont spec. The same algorithm and the same 328 .flf fonts as patorjk's TAAG generator, vendored as the byte source of truth — so output is byte-identical, not a lookalike.

Every font is a content-addressed κ-object. Fonts are lazy (only the one you pick is ever fetched) and each .flf is re-verified against its sealed κ on read (Law L5). Zero CDN, zero foreign runtime (Law L4).

ul: Layout control: smushing · fitted · full width
Shows the active font's dual-axis κ

releases.description: FIGlet / TAAG, natively encoded: a κ-addressable text→ASCII encoder over the vendored figlet core (Patrick Gillespie, MIT) + 328 .flf fonts. Lazy, content-addressed fonts re-verified on read (Law L5); no CDN (Law L4). Live preview, type-to-filter font picker, layout control, copy/download, and the active font's dual-axis κ. The engine is also wired OS-wide as HoloFX.ascii(text, {font}).
